error.signature: Cloud Run request takes 30s+ on first invocation due to large container image

verdict.summary: Cloud Run pulls the container image on cold start. Images >1GB can take 10-30s+ to pull. Google's docs recommend small images but don't quantify the cold start penalty per image size.

dead_ends.action: Set min-instances=1 and assume zero cold starts
dead_ends.why_fails: min-instances keeps 1 instance warm, but concurrent request spikes still trigger cold starts for additional instances.

dead_ends.action: Use a health check endpoint to keep instances warm
dead_ends.why_fails: Cloud Run auto-scales to zero regardless of health checks. There's no built-in keep-alive mechanism.

workarounds.action: Use multi-stage Docker builds to minimize image size
workarounds.how: FROM golang:1.21 AS builder ... FROM gcr.io/distroless/static ... COPY --from=builder /app /app

workarounds.action: Set min-instances > 0 AND set CPU to 'always allocated' for consistent performance
Use startup CPU boost to reduce cold start latency
workarounds.how: gcloud run deploy --cpu-boost  # doubles CPU during startup for faster init
